question: For the `Point` class (from a previous problem), overload the prefix increment `++` operator. It should increment both the `x` and `y` coordinates of the `Point` object and return a reference to the modified object.

test_cases.input: Initial Point p1(1,2); call ++p1; check p1;
Initial Point p2(5,5); call returned_p2 = ++p2; check p2 and returned_p2; modify returned_p2.x = 10; check p2 and returned_p2;
test_cases.expected:
After ++p2, p2: (6, 6)
Returned object: (6, 6)
After returned_p2.x = 10, p2: (6, 6)
After returned_p2.x = 10, returned_p2: (10, 6)

test_cases.input: Initial Point p3(10,20); call ++(++p3) (simulated); check p3 and the value of the last increment;
test_cases.expected:
Initial p3: (10, 20)
After ++(++p3) operation, p3: (11, 21)
Value of ++(++p3) (last returned temp): (12, 22)

errors: The prefix increment operator is incorrectly defined to return a `Point` object by value instead of a `Point&` (reference). While the original object is incremented, the return value is a copy. This leads to incorrect behavior when chaining increments (only the first increment affects the original object) or when modifying the returned object (modifications won't affect the original `Point`).
